title: use 4.0.2 as version - this is a maintenance branch
body:
[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

Signed-off-by: Jindrich Novy <jnovy@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *jnovy*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v4.0/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Shouldn't there be a 4.0-rhel branch?
I think the 4.0 branch should be used for normal upstream usage.
@Luap99 @mheon I'm fine with both, e.g. v4.0-rhel sounds good too. Just need it quite quickly as I need a source of truth for the exception granted bug and RHEL work.
I am fine with that also, although I am not sure we are going to differentiate between the two of them, especially once 4.1 is released.
NACK from my side.  RHEL should use a `-rhel` suffixed branch - which does not exist yet.

> I am fine with that also, although I am not sure we are going to differentiate between the two of them, especially once 4.1 is released.

RHEL should always use a `-rhel` branch for us not having to ask (or answer) this questions :^) This way, we'll make sure not to screw things up.
How about we create a new branch from commit https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/342c8259381b63296e96ad29519bd4b9c7afbf97 and call it `v4.0-rhel`
The new v.4.0-rhel branch must be at the state of tip of v4.0 (ec2fdb1350c12b2df6aae8189ab97f3b0e94bb8e) without https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/0f626c377aac8f469c8889bdce3ca366ca7ff121

> The new v.4.0-rhel branch must be at the state of tip of v4.0 ([ec2fdb1](https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/ec2fdb1350c12b2df6aae8189ab97f3b0e94bb8e)) without [0f626c3](https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/0f626c377aac8f469c8889bdce3ca366ca7ff121)

I pushed a v4.0-rhel branch upstream with this change. PTAL.

> > The new v.4.0-rhel branch must be at the state of tip of v4.0 ([ec2fdb1](https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/ec2fdb1350c12b2df6aae8189ab97f3b0e94bb8e)) without [0f626c3](https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/0f626c377aac8f469c8889bdce3ca366ca7ff121)
> 
> I pushed a v4.0-rhel branch upstream with this change. PTAL.

@vrothberg @Luap99 @mheon @rhatdan PTAL at the new branch as well.

LGTM
I've rerun the failing test, some kind of network timeout looking for a gateway.

> LGTM I've rerun the failing test, some kind of network timeout looking for a gateway.

Closing this PR. The v4.0-rhel branch should be good to go for rhel builds.  Please reopen if that was a mistake.
I just realized that one thing's off: the RHEL branch should be named `v4.0.2-rhel` to be consistent with other branches but I don't think it's a big deal.

> I just realized that one thing's off: the RHEL branch should be named `v4.0.2-rhel` to be consistent with other branches but I don't think it's a big deal.

ack, I created the new branch being under the impression that rhel could get future v4.0 releases, my bad. I could push a new `v4.0.2-rhel` if we *need* it.

I'd leave it as it is @lsm5 - based on previous experience maintaining older branches. It might happen that we introduce higher version to v4.0.2-rhel, e.g. 4.0.3 (we did that). Then the branch name would start to be confusing. Maybe we should reconsider the upstream RHEL branch naming policy to only vX.Y-rhel @vrothberg ? I can't possibly see any benefit of vX.Y.Z-rhel off top on my head at the moment. Let's discuss it.
Yeah, let's leave it. Misnamed branches aren't really a problem.
